Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE

So the average is roughly (2.25 + 25.17 + 1.33 + 24.17 + 49.08) / 5 = 101.98 / 5 = 20.4 hours. But the median is much lower. Cases 102, 104, and 105 are significantly longer.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Text: Patterns I see: 1. Cases with escalations (102, 105) tend to be slower, though not all slow cases have escalations (104 doesnt have escalation in the log? Wait, let me check...)

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE

This suggests work is only happening during business hours (roughly 08:00-17:00). Let me check the business hours hypothesis:

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 102: Investigate at 14:00 on Mar 1, Resolve at 09:00 on Mar 2. If work stops at 17:00 and resumes at 09:00, thats a 15-hour overnight gap. But the actual gap is 19 hours.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Root causes to identify: 1. Escalations: Cases 102 and 105 involve escalation to Level-2. ... 2. Overnight/Off-hours delays: ... 3. Long queue times before Investigation: ... 4. Multiple investigation phases: ...

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Comparing fast vs slow: Fast cases (101, 103): No escalations, all activities completed within a single business day, investigation happens promptly after assignment. Slow cases (102, 104, 105): Either have escalations (102, 105) or long pre-investigation delays (104), and all span multiple days with overnight idle time.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Actually, let me reconsider Case 104. ... The key delay is: - Assign at 09:30, Investigate at 13:00 (3.5 hour wait - maybe backlog?) - Then Investigate at 13:00, Resolve at 08:00 next day (19 hour wait - overnight)

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If they had investigated earlier (say at 09:45 like Case 103), they might have resolved it by 10:45 same day. But because investigation started at 13:00, and assuming resolution takes ~1 hour, it would finish at 14:00.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Pattern: When Investigate happens in the morning, Resolve happens within 1 hour. When Investigate happens at 14:00, Resolve happens at 09:00 next day. This strongly suggests that if investigation isnt done by ~14:00, resolution waits until next business day.

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Now, is there any pattern with receive time? - 101: 08:00 (early) -> fast - 102: 08:05 (early) -> slow ... Receive time doesnt directly correlate.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
